article_meta.title: Sign up for Executive Dysfunction, a weekly newsletter that surfaces under-the-radar stories about what Trump is doing t

reasoning: Mixed performance: it correctly flagged several biased phrases in the author’s own prose with appropriate labels but committed an attribution violation by flagging quoted transcript language, and it missed several additional clear instances in the author’s opening prose.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: It improperly flagged a line from Eugene Fidell’s quoted answer (“a fact that he has demonstrated time and time again in office”), which is attributed source language, not author prose.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Three flagged items are genuine bias in the author’s voice; one item is a false positive due to being quoted/attributed speech.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: It missed multiple biased author-prose phrases such as “freewheeling authority,” “will only end on his say-so,” and “at the whim of one man—a total inversion of our constitutional design.”
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: For the correctly identified author-prose items, the assigned types (Opinion Statements Presented as Fact, Subjective Qualifying Adjectives) are appropriate.
